author: @un_skilled
text: I realize this is an older video. Maybe I miss but what does "FWG" stand for? I realize their abbreviations. Can you please inform me.

replies.author: @djmips
replies.text: FWG was a technology startup to capitilize on graphene based products.  The intials FWG stood for "First Working Generation"

author: @luckybrand5013

author: @zylascope
text: Good work FWG!

author: @alanh4471
text:
YT, Hopefully the best patent you can get.

Would be interesting if a 'major' could corporately copy your companies ideas and fight it in a court, as in the YT proof shows your trials and tribulations...

Be aware though, a real big major could cost you lots of money to defend it. Remember Hoover vs Dyson? Dyson won because ultimately he had the money.

You're dealing with the future battery storage tech that the planet earth will be moving into. There's billions, if not $Trillions$ at stake here.
Many hundreds of $BILLIONS$ are involved in the Lithium mining/battery industry. You might make them go broke...!

Maybe you'll get a pay out. A rock solid NDA. $100mill and disappear off social media....

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: some to worry about if it happens i feel

author: @felipepicard8181
text: Hey, Robert, you kind of misspelled Patti's email. You pressed space bar after "@fwgltd". Just letting you know so you can change it, because some people may not understand why their emails aren't being sent.

author: @Khwartz
text: What An Awesome Good Surprised to discover that You're not only developing Green  Cheap Technology but that You also Spread Your Enthusiasm for Sciences, en especially for graphene! :D :D :D  Really Love You All Guys! <3 <3 <3

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: Thanks for the kind words - but we really want to do as much as we can for free to encourage as many as possible to share in the excitement of discovery!

author: @AaronHarper
text: Absolutely brilliant folks!  Rob, once again, we are alike.  I tutor mathematics and science, sponsoring the exploration of young minds, and Jaye is president of the school board here, working at a policy level.  I have forwarded this video and Patti's contact information to those who will use it well. :)

author: @SpeakerWiggin49
text: Pointing to the bottom of the screen in exaggerated motions is the correct way to refer people to information in the description. It needs to be this way to stick in people's minds, and it's reminiscent of exaggerated indicative motions in play performance.
